prompt:

Sample Dialogue:
Imogen: "You weren't the operator I expected. But you're the one the room wanted."

Photoreal live-action shot continuing from the seed frame of an empty 1974 film cutting room. A real woman in her mid-40s — tired film editor, unstyled brown hair with grey at the temples, no makeup, visible pores and under-eye shadows, worn flannel shirt over a plain tee, practical pants — pushes open the stage-left door with her shoulder, walks in carrying a ceramic mug like she has done this a thousand times. She crosses to the desk, sets the mug down with a dull ceramic click, turns toward camera. She looks slightly past the lens first, then meets it, and speaks quietly, dry, close-mic, almost under her breath — not theatrical. Natural body weight, slight hesitation before she speaks, imperfect timing. Locked tripod, available tungsten practical only, long hard shadows, heavy 16mm grain, soft focus, 24fps documentary. No beauty lighting, no glow, no VFX.
Sample Dialogue:
Imogen (quiet, dry): "You weren't the operator I expected. But you're the one the room wanted."

Camera remains static and locked throughout — same wide tripod framing and distance to the desk as the input. NO zoom, NO push-in, NO dolly, NO track, NO pan, NO tilt, NO close-up. Same Steenbeck with two reels, Royal typewriter with page, coffee mug, Bolex on tripod, open coastal window. Do not add objects. Do not relocate props. No fog, no thick white vapor, no cotton clouds, no new lamps, no green walls. Only the parts listed under MOVES may change. Motion continues naturally to the final frame — do not freeze, settle, or hold still at the end.
